tasks.title: Create profile data model, state management, and core profile form
tasks.description:
Create the core profile infrastructure for the portfolio app. Read `src/App.jsx`, `src/pages/About.jsx`, `src/pages/About.css`, `src/main.jsx`, and `src/components/Navbar.jsx` to understand existing structure and conventions.

1. Create `src/context/ProfileContext.jsx` â€” a React Context that manages profile state using localStorage for persistence. The profile shape should include: { name, headline, bio (max 500 chars), location, email, phone, website, photoUrl, socialLinks, skills }. Provide a `saveProfile` function that writes to localStorage.

2. Create `src/pages/Profile.jsx` â€” a new page component (following the pattern of `src/pages/About.jsx`) that renders:
   - A profile edit form when in edit mode
   - Fields: name (text), headline (text), bio (textarea, 500 char limit with live counter), location (text), email (text), phone (text), website (text)
   - Save and Cancel buttons
   - An 'Edit Profile' button when not in edit mode
   - A 'Preview Profile' button to toggle preview mode (implemented later in REQ-005)

3. Create `src/pages/Profile.css` following the patterns in `src/pages/About.css`.

4. Wrap the app with ProfileProvider in `src/main.jsx`.

5. Add a `/profile` route to `src/App.jsx` and add a 'Profile' nav link to `src/components/Navbar.jsx` following the existing nav link pattern.

6. Write tests in `src/pages/Profile.test.jsx` following patterns from `src/sections/Contact.test.jsx`. Test: form renders with all fields, bio character counter works and enforces 500 char limit, save button writes to localStorage, cancel button discards unsaved changes.
acceptance_criteria: ProfileContext provides profile state and saveProfile function via React Context
Profile page renders an edit form with name, headline, bio, location, email, phone, website fields
Bio textarea enforces 500-character limit with a live counter display
Save writes to localStorage within 2 seconds
Cancel discards unsaved changes and reverts to saved state
/profile route is accessible and linked in the Navbar
Tests pass for form rendering, field input, char counter, save, and cancel behaviors

tasks.title: Implement profile photo upload with client-side resizing to 400x400px
tasks.description:
Create a profile photo upload component that resizes images client-side. Read `src/pages/Profile.jsx` and `src/context/ProfileContext.jsx` (created in REQ-001) before starting.

1. Create `src/components/ProfilePhoto/ProfilePhoto.jsx` â€” a component that:
   - Displays the current profile photo from ProfileContext, or a default SVG avatar placeholder if none is set
   - Has an upload overlay/button that appears on hover
   - Uses a hidden `<input type='file' accept='image/*'>` for file selection
   - On file select, uses the Canvas API to resize the image: center-crop to a square, then scale to 400x400px
   - Converts the canvas to a base64 data URL and calls `saveProfile` from ProfileContext to persist `photoUrl`
   - Shows a loading indicator while processing the image

2. Create `src/components/ProfilePhoto/ProfilePhoto.css`.

3. Integrate `<ProfilePhoto />` into `src/pages/Profile.jsx` at the top of the profile form section.

4. Write tests in `src/components/ProfilePhoto/ProfilePhoto.test.jsx`. Mock `HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext` and file reading. Test: renders placeholder SVG when no photoUrl, upload button triggers file input click, component calls saveProfile with a data URL after file selection.
acceptance_criteria: ProfilePhoto renders a default placeholder when no photo is set
Clicking the upload button/overlay triggers the hidden file input
Selected image is center-cropped and resized to 400x400px using Canvas API
Resized image is saved as a base64 data URL via ProfileContext saveProfile
Loading state is shown during image processing
ProfilePhoto component is rendered in the Profile page form
Tests pass for placeholder rendering, upload trigger, and save behavior

tasks.title: Implement social media links manager with automatic platform icon recognition
tasks.description:
Create a social media links manager component. Read `src/pages/Profile.jsx` and `src/context/ProfileContext.jsx` before starting.

1. Create `src/components/SocialLinks/SocialLinks.jsx` â€” a component that:
   - Displays up to 10 social media links, each with a URL text input and auto-detected platform label
   - Detects the platform from the URL hostname: github.comâ†’GitHub, linkedin.comâ†’LinkedIn, twitter.com/x.comâ†’Twitter/X, instagram.comâ†’Instagram, youtube.comâ†’YouTube, facebook.comâ†’Facebook, dribbble.comâ†’Dribbble, behance.netâ†’Behance, stackoverflow.comâ†’Stack Overflow, dev.toâ†’Dev.to, and 'Link' as fallback
   - Has an 'Add Link' button that appends a new empty link entry (disabled when 10 links are already added)
   - Each link row has a Remove button that deletes that entry
   - On change/blur, the updated links array is saved via ProfileContext `saveProfile`

2. Create `src/components/SocialLinks/SocialLinks.css`.

3. Integrate `<SocialLinks />` into `src/pages/Profile.jsx` within the edit form.

4. Write tests in `src/components/SocialLinks/SocialLinks.test.jsx`. Test: renders empty state with Add Link button, clicking Add Link shows a new URL input, entering a GitHub URL auto-labels it 'GitHub', Add button is disabled when 10 links exist, Remove button deletes the corresponding link entry.
acceptance_criteria: SocialLinks renders existing links and an Add Link button
Platform label auto-detected from URL hostname for at least 10 known platforms plus a fallback
Maximum of 10 links enforced â€” Add Link button is disabled when limit is reached
Remove button deletes the corresponding link
Link changes are saved to ProfileContext
SocialLinks is rendered in the Profile page form
Tests pass for all described behaviors

tasks.title: Implement skills input with autocomplete suggestions
tasks.description:
Create a skills input component with dropdown autocomplete. Read `src/pages/Profile.jsx` and `src/context/ProfileContext.jsx` before starting.

1. Create `src/components/SkillsInput/SkillsInput.jsx` â€” a component that:
   - Displays currently added skills as removable tag chips
   - Has a text input; as the user types, filters a predefined skills list and shows a dropdown of matching suggestions
   - Predefined list must include at minimum: JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Vue, Angular, Node.js, Python, Java, CSS, HTML, GraphQL, REST API, Git, Docker, AWS, SQL, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Redis, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Jest, Figma, DevOps, CI/CD
   - Clicking a suggestion or pressing Enter adds the skill as a tag and clears the input
   - Pressing Escape closes the dropdown without adding a skill
   - Each tag has an X button to remove the skill
   - Duplicate skills are silently ignored
   - Skills array is saved to ProfileContext on every change

2. Create `src/components/SkillsInput/SkillsInput.css`.

3. Integrate `<SkillsInput />` into `src/pages/Profile.jsx` within the edit form.

4. Write tests in `src/components/SkillsInput/SkillsInput.test.jsx`. Test: existing skills render as tags, typing 'React' shows React in dropdown, clicking a suggestion adds the tag and clears input, pressing Enter adds the tag, pressing Escape closes dropdown, clicking X on a tag removes it, adding a duplicate skill is ignored.
acceptance_criteria: Skills display as removable tag chips
Typing filters the predefined suggestion list in real time
Clicking a suggestion or pressing Enter adds the skill and clears the input
Pressing Escape closes the dropdown without adding a skill
Duplicate skills are not added
X button on each tag removes that skill
Skills array is persisted via ProfileContext
SkillsInput is rendered in the Profile page form
Tests pass for all described behaviors

tasks.title: Implement public profile preview mode
tasks.description:
Create a read-only profile preview and wire up the preview toggle in the Profile page. Read `src/pages/Profile.jsx`, `src/context/ProfileContext.jsx`, `src/components/ProfilePhoto/ProfilePhoto.jsx`, `src/components/SocialLinks/SocialLinks.jsx`, and `src/components/SkillsInput/SkillsInput.jsx` before starting.

1. Create `src/components/ProfilePreview/ProfilePreview.jsx` â€” a read-only display component that:
   - Reads all profile data from ProfileContext
   - Displays: profile photo (or placeholder), name, headline, bio, location, email, phone, website
   - Displays social links as clickable `<a>` elements with `target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer'` and their platform labels
   - Displays skills as non-interactive tag chips (no X button, no input)
   - Contains zero form inputs or edit controls

2. Create `src/components/ProfilePreview/ProfilePreview.css`.

3. Update `src/pages/Profile.jsx` to add a preview toggle:
   - Add a 'Preview Profile' button visible in edit mode that switches to preview mode showing `<ProfilePreview />`
   - Add a 'Back to Edit' button in preview mode that returns to the edit form
   - The toggle does not affect saved data

4. Write tests in `src/components/ProfilePreview/ProfilePreview.test.jsx`: renders name, headline, bio, location from context, social links render as anchor elements with correct href, skills render as non-interactive chips, no form inputs are present.

5. Add tests to `src/pages/Profile.test.jsx` for the toggle: clicking 'Preview Profile' renders ProfilePreview, clicking 'Back to Edit' renders the form again.
acceptance_criteria: ProfilePreview renders photo, name, headline, bio, location, email, phone, website from ProfileContext
Social links render as clickable anchor tags opening in a new tab
Skills render as read-only tag chips with no interactive controls
No form inputs or edit controls appear in ProfilePreview
'Preview Profile' button in edit mode switches to preview mode
'Back to Edit' button in preview mode returns to the edit form
Tests pass for preview rendering and toggle behavior

tasks.title: Validate all profile feature tests pass with no regressions
tasks.description:
Run the full test suite to validate the profile feature and check for regressions.

1. Run each new test file individually to get clear output:
   - `npx vitest run src/pages/Profile.test.jsx`
   - `npx vitest run src/components/ProfilePhoto/ProfilePhoto.test.jsx`
   - `npx vitest run src/components/SocialLinks/SocialLinks.test.jsx`
   - `npx vitest run src/components/SkillsInput/SkillsInput.test.jsx`
   - `npx vitest run src/components/ProfilePreview/ProfilePreview.test.jsx`
2. Run the full suite for regression checks: `npx vitest run`
3. Report any failures with full test names and error messages.
4. Do NOT modify production code. Only fix test files if the test itself has a clear bug (wrong query, missing mock). Report any production code bugs found.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 new test files pass with 0 failures
No regressions in existing tests (Home, About, Projects, Skills, Contact, Navbar, Footer, App)
npx vitest run exits with code 0
